title: Allow agents to add queue items
description: Agents can add new items to queue (e.g., 'I found another issue'). Needs agent tools/skills for queue access. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Agent Capabilities
close_reason: agents can invoke sq add

title: Add notifications
description: Alert when background agents finish: terminal bell, desktop notification, or status line update. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Open Questions
close_reason: not important

title: Research custom Roast cog implementation
description: Dig into Roast::Cog base class to understand what's needed for a custom 'human' cog that pauses workflow, queues item for review, waits for decision. Look at: Cog::Config, Cog::Input, Cog::Output, execution lifecycle.
notes:
## Research Findings

### Custom Cog Structure
- Subclass `Roast::Cog`
- Define `Config`, `Input`, `Output` inner classes
- Implement `execute(input) -> Output`
- Register via `use [:cog_name], from: 'path'` in workflow

### Input Lifecycle
1. `Input.new` (no args)
2. Input block sets values
3. `validate!` called
4. If invalid, `coerce(return_value)` called
5. `validate!` again
6. `execute(input)` called

### Blocking Mechanism
Cog runs in `Async::Task`. Options for human-in-the-loop:
- Queue + Signal (Async::Queue)
- File watch
- Socket/HTTP callback
- DRb or similar IPC

### Next Steps
- Prototype a minimal `human` cog
- Test blocking behavior with Async
- Design Sift ↔ Cog communication protocol
close_reason: Researched Roast cog extension points. Custom cogs: subclass Roast::Cog, define Config/Input/Output, implement execute(). Register via 'use' DSL. Runs in Async::Task - can block on Queue/Signal for human input.

title: Choose TUI library
description: Options: tty-prompt, cli-ui (Shopify), curses, reline. Need: hotkeys, text input, scrollable content, drill-down views. Research and pick one.
close_reason: Decision: cli-ui (same as Roast). Installed via Gemfile.

title: Design agent tools for queue access
description: What should agents be able to do with the queue? Read current item, add new items, mark complete. CLI commands vs MCP tools vs skill files?
close_reason: Deferred: Prototype uses in-memory queue in ReviewLoop. Agent queue access (MCP tools, CLI commands) can be added when we need persistent queues or multi-agent workflows.

title: Add batch operations
description: Hotkeys for bulk actions: 'A' approve all remaining, 'R' reject all remaining. Maybe filter first then batch. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Open Questions
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: update transcript renderer to support Claude Code JSONL format
description:
The transcript source renderer currently only supports plaintext files with 'Human:'/'Assistant:'/'System:' speaker prefixes (one per line).

Claude Code stores conversations as JSONL with a different structure:
- Each line is a JSON object with a 'type' field ('user', 'assistant', or 'queue-operation')
- User entries: message.content is an array of {type: 'text', text: '...'} parts
- Assistant entries: message.content is an array mixing 'text' parts and 'tool_use' parts

The renderer should detect JSONL transcript files (by extension or by content sniffing the first line) and extract the human/assistant text accordingly, so real Claude Code session transcripts can be viewed in the review queue.

A fixture file exists at test/fixtures/sources/claude_code_conversation.jsonl for testing.

title: Enhance diff source renderer
description: Enhance diff renderer with line numbers, width-aware truncation, and hunk header display. Current Source::Diff only applies ANSI colors (+green, -red, @cyan) with no line numbers, no width handling, and no structured hunk header rendering. Should add: (1) old/new line number gutter parsed from @@ headers, (2) width-aware line truncation, (3) better hunk header formatting showing function context, (4) consider side-by-side mode for wide terminals. The render(width:, height:) interface is already in place.

title: Source navigation and drill-down views
description:
Navigate between multiple sources within a queue item and drill into details.

## Source Navigation
- `n` / `→` - Next source
- `p` / `←` - Previous source  
- Status bar shows: `[2/4] file: src/foo.rb`

## Type-Aware Rendering
Each source type has custom display:

**diff**: Colored +/- lines, line numbers, file header
**file**: Syntax highlighting (via Rouge or similar), line numbers
**transcript**: Dialog format with speaker labels, timestamps
**text**: Markdown rendering or plain text

## Drill-down Views
- `d` - Full file diff (all hunks for current file, not just this one)
- `t` - Full transcript (complete Claude session history)
- `f` - File browser (list all sources, jump to any)

## Implementation
- Extract rendering logic from ReviewLoop into Source renderers
- Each source type: `Sift::Source::Diff`, `Sift::Source::File`, etc.
- Common interface: `#render(width:, height:)` returns formatted string

Depends on: n89 (queue model defines source structure)
close_reason: Source renderers, navigation, viewer, smoke tests

title: Design the workflow DSL
description: Refine the Ruby DSL sketch for defining workflows. Key questions: source/context/analyze/on_approve/on_revise blocks - is this the right structure? How does it compare to Roast's DSL?
close_reason: Deferred: Current ReviewLoop class is simple enough without DSL. Add DSL when we need configurable workflows (different sources, custom analyze prompts, etc.)

title: Wire up review loop
description: Connect TUI + Claude: load diff hunks from ~/.dotfiles, analyze each, show response, handle approve/reject/revise actions, move to next item. In-memory queue only.
close_reason: ReviewLoop wires TUI + ClaudeClient + DiffParser: loads hunks from git, analyzes each with Claude, displays diff+analysis, handles a/r/c/v/q actions with session continuity for revisions

title: Add Ctrl+G for nvim
description: When typing revision feedback, Ctrl+G opens $EDITOR for complex/multiline feedback. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Revision input' decision

title: Implement sift_queue cog for Roast
description:
Custom Roast cog that pushes items to Sift queue from within a workflow.

## Use Case
Workflow is analyzing code, finds something that needs human review mid-workflow:

```ruby
# In workflow.rb
agent!(:analyze) do
  # ... analysis prompt ...
end

sift_queue do
  sources [
    { type: 'text', content: agent!(:analyze).response },
    { type: 'diff', path: target }
  ]
  metadata workflow: 'analyze', step: 'initial'
end

# Workflow continues...
```

## Cog Implementation
```ruby
module Sift
  module Cogs
    class SiftQueue < Roast::Cog
      class Input
        attr_accessor :sources, :metadata, :queue_path
      end
      
      class Output
        attr_accessor :item_id
      end
      
      def execute(input)
        queue = Sift::Queue.new(input.queue_path || '.sift/queue.jsonl')
        item = queue.push(
          sources: input.sources,
          metadata: input.metadata
        )
        Output.new(item_id: item.id)
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Registration
```ruby
# In workflow
use [:sift_queue], from: 'sift/cogs'
```

## Difference from atu
- atu: Sift calls Roast, captures final output
- 6ep: Roast calls Sift mid-workflow to queue items

Both are needed:
- atu for "run this workflow and review results"
- 6ep for "workflow decides what needs human review"

## Dependencies
- n89: Queue API must exist
- atu: May share queue instance/configuration
close_reason: Already implemented as sift_output cog in atu. Same functionality: workflow pushes to Sift queue via cog.

close_reason: Scope defined. See EXPLORATION.md 'Prototype Scope (v0)' section.

title: Add queue filtering
description: Filter queue view by status (pending/approved/rejected/failed) or type. Show subset of items. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Open Questions

title: sift: Migrate exe/sift to CLI::Base framework
description:
The exe/sift entry point currently uses a raw OptionParser inline in the script. It should be refactored to use the same Sift::CLI::Base subcommand-per-class pattern established for sq.

## Current State
exe/sift has inline OptionParser with --queue, --model, --version, --help flags. It directly instantiates Sift::Queue and Sift::ReviewLoop.

## Target State
A Sift::CLI::SiftCommand < Base class that:
- Uses command_name, summary, description, examples
- Defines flags via define_flags (--queue, --model)
- Handles --version
- Calls ReviewLoop in execute
- exe/sift becomes a one-liner: exit Sift::CLI::SiftCommand.new(ARGV).run

## Patterns to Follow
See lib/sift/cli/base.rb and lib/sift/cli/queue_command.rb for the established pattern. Key conventions:
- command_name/summary/description/examples class-level metadata
- define_flags(parser, options) with super chain
- execute returns exit code
- HelpRenderer provides gh-style help output

## Reference
- exe/sift (current inline implementation)
- exe/sq (already uses CLI::Base via QueueCommand)
- lib/sift/cli/base.rb (the framework)
close_reason: Migrated exe/sift to CLI::Base framework. SiftCommand class with define_flags/execute pattern, exe/sift is now a one-liner.

title: Integrate Roast wrapper orchestration
description:
Sift orchestrates Roast workflows externally, captures output, queues results for human review.

## Orchestrator API
```ruby
orchestrator = Sift::Roast::Orchestrator.new(
  workflow: 'workflows/analyze.rb',
  queue: Sift::Queue.new('.sift/queue.jsonl')
)

# Run workflow, queue results
orchestrator.run(
  targets: ['src/foo.rb', 'src/bar.rb'],
  kwargs: { context: 'refactoring' }
)

# Run revision workflow with feedback
orchestrator.revise(
  item_id: 'abc123',
  feedback: 'Focus more on performance implications'
)
```

## Output Capture
Roast::Workflow.from_file returns void, so capture via:
- File-based: Workflow writes to temp file, orchestrator reads it
- Custom output cog: Workflow uses `sift_output` cog that writes to known location
- Alternatively: Modify Roast to return workflow result

## Queue Integration
When workflow completes:
1. Parse output (structured or free-form)
2. Create queue item with sources:
   - transcript: workflow conversation
   - text: workflow output/analysis
   - file/diff: original targets
3. Push to queue with metadata (workflow name, run timestamp)

## Revision Flow
1. Human provides feedback on queue item
2. Orchestrator runs revision workflow with:
   - Original context (from item metadata)
   - Human feedback (as kwarg)
   - Session continuity if supported
3. New/updated item appears in queue

## Dependencies
- n89: Queue must exist to push results
- Roast gem: Need to understand from_file API better
close_reason: Roast wrapper orchestrator: run() and revise() methods, sift_output custom cog for workflows to push to queue via SIFT_QUEUE_PATH env var

title: Wire ReviewLoop to queue items
description: Refactor ReviewLoop to read from queue instead of git diff. Currently ReviewLoop::load_hunks calls DiffParser.from_git directly and iterates over raw hunks. It should instead: (1) accept a Queue instance (or path), (2) iterate over pending queue items via queue.each_pending, (3) for each item, use SourceViewer to render the item's sources (diff, file, transcript, text), (4) on approve/reject, update the queue item status, (5) preserve the existing session continuity for revise flow using item.session_id. The SourceViewer module already handles multi-source navigation with next/prev/jump and drill-downs - ReviewLoop should include it. The current prompt_action keybindings and handle_action logic can stay mostly intact, just wired to queue operations instead of GitActions.
close_reason: Refactored ReviewLoop to be queue-driven. Now accepts queue: instead of path:/base:, includes SourceViewer for multi-source rendering, updates queue item status on approve/reject, persists session_id for Claude continuity, and adds n/p/s keys for source navigation. 15 new tests added. All 163 tests pass.

title: Add parallel/background agents
description: Run agent analysis in background while human reviews. Queue coordinates: when agent finishes, result appears in queue. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Architecture Diagram

title: Decide: Roast extension vs standalone
description: Three options: extend Roast with queue/input cogs, build on top of Roast, or standalone with cherry-picked ideas. Queue-driven model is fundamentally different from Roast's step-execution model.
close_reason: Decision: Standalone for prototype. Clean separation, simpler to iterate. Can integrate with Roast later if queue model proves valuable.

title: sq: Add validation and schema for queue items
description:
Validation gaps in the queue:

1. Queue#update accepts arbitrary **attrs and merges via Item.new(**item.to_h.merge(attrs)) — no validation that keys are known fields. A typo like staus: 'approved' silently creates a broken item.
2. No validation that path-based sources (diff, file, transcript) reference existing files.
3. Source.from_h does not validate type — only push does via validate_sources!. Direct Item.from_h from JSONL can load items with invalid source types.

We should define a proper schema for the JSONL structure. Consider using an IDL or schema definition (e.g., JSON Schema, dry-schema, or a custom DSL) to validate items on read and write.

title: Understand Claude CLI session mechanics
description: How does claude --resume work? Does it fit our sticky-session-per-item model? What's the session ID format? Can we fork sessions?
close_reason: Mechanics verified. See EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics' section.

title: feat: add --dry-mode flag to replace Claude calls with no-op output
description: Add a --dry-mode flag to the sift CLI that replaces actual Claude API calls with a no-op. In dry mode, instead of calling Claude, print what would be sent (the prompt text, model, session info). This enables testing the review flow end-to-end without incurring API costs or needing a Claude CLI installation.

title: Enhance file source renderer
description: Enhance file source renderer with syntax-aware display. Current Source::File renders line numbers with a gray gutter but no syntax awareness - just raw text. Should add: (1) file extension detection for syntax-appropriate display, (2) width-aware line truncation (currently ignores width param), (3) highlight regions of interest if metadata specifies line ranges, (4) consider basic keyword coloring for common languages (Ruby, JS, JSON). Keep it simple - not full syntax highlighting, just enough to orient the reviewer.

title: Pick a name for the tool
description: Candidates: review-loop, human-loop, hl, queue-review, agent-queue, signal, sift. Want something short, memorable, conveys human-in-the-loop concept.
close_reason: Decision: 'sift' - short, memorable, conveys filtering/reviewing concept. Renaming bin/roast-review to bin/sift.

title: Integrate Claude CLI for analysis
description: Call claude -p with diff hunk, capture response and session_id from JSON output. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics'
close_reason: ClaudeClient class: prompt(), analyze_diff(), session_id capture and resumption via --resume flag

title: sq: Add file locking for concurrent queue access
description:
The JSONL queue file has no file locking. Concurrent sq processes can corrupt the file through interleaved writes or lost updates during read-modify-write cycles in update/remove.

Implement flock-based file locking to ensure safe concurrent access to the queue file. Both append_item (push) and write_all (update/remove/clear) need protection.

title: Wire up accept/reject actions
description: Accept → stage that hunk (git add -p equivalent). Reject → revert that hunk (git checkout -p equivalent). Currently decisions array just counts for summary - make it actually do something.
close_reason: Accept → git apply --cached (stages hunk). Reject → git apply --reverse (reverts hunk). Added GitActions module, updated DiffParser to capture full patch.

title: Make Claude analysis optional
description: Show diff first, user decides. Add '?' hotkey to ask Claude for analysis. Current flow calls Claude automatically - new flow: show diff → user decides (a/r/c/?) → '?' triggers Claude analysis
close_reason: Show diff first, '?' triggers Claude analysis. Prompt changes based on whether analysis exists. 'v' revise only works after analysis.

title: Implement workflow DSL
description: Ruby DSL for defining workflows: source (where items come from), context (what to show), analyze (prompt template), on_approve/on_reject hooks. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Workflow DSL Sketch'
close_reason: Consolidating on Roast integration (atu + 6ep). Native DSL would duplicate Roast's capabilities.

title: Persist queue to JSONL
description:
JSONL queue persistence with multi-source items.

## Queue Item Model
```json
{
  "id": "abc123",
  "status": "pending",
  "sources": [
    {"type": "diff", "path": "src/foo.rb", "content": "..."},
    {"type": "file", "path": "src/foo_test.rb"},
    {"type": "transcript", "session_id": "uuid", "content": "..."},
    {"type": "text", "content": "Review notes here"}
  ],
  "metadata": {},
  "session_id": null,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}
```

## Source Types
- **diff**: Git hunk with colored +/- display
- **file**: Path reference (lazy load) or inline content
- **transcript**: Claude conversation history
- **text**: Freeform markdown/plain text

## Status Values
- pending, in_progress, approved, rejected, failed

## Queue API (Ruby)
```ruby
queue = Sift::Queue.new('.sift/queue.jsonl')
queue.push(sources: [...], metadata: {...})
queue.each_pending { |item| ... }
queue.update(id, status: :approved)
queue.filter(status: :pending)
```

## File Format
- One JSON object per line (JSONL)
- Append-only writes, periodic compaction
- Git-friendly (merge conflicts rare)
close_reason: Queue foundation implemented: Item/Source structs, JSONL persistence, full API (push/update/find/filter/count/remove/clear), status helpers, validation

title: sq: Harden ID generation
description:
Issues with current ID generation:

1. generate_id calls all() on every push, re-parsing the entire JSONL file — O(n) per insert
2. 3-char alphanumeric (a-z0-9) gives only 46,656 possible IDs — collision pressure grows fast
3. The loop has no bound or safety exit if the space is exhausted

Note: Research how Beads (bd) does its ID generation — it uses a similar short-ID scheme and may have solved these problems already.

title: sq: Refactor CLI to subcommand-per-class architecture
description:
The current sq CLI uses a single QueueCommand class with send("run_\#{subcommand}") dispatch. This needs a full architectural overhaul:

## Requirements

1. Each subcommand MUST be its own class (AddCommand, EditCommand, ListCommand, ShowCommand, RmCommand)
2. Help output should follow the gh CLI model:
   - Core vs Additional command grouping
   - FLAGS section with explicit flag listing
   - INHERITED FLAGS showing parent-parsed flags
   - EXAMPLES section
   - LEARN MORE section
   - Subcommands can have subcommands (nested)
   - ARGUMENTS section where relevant
3. Parent command parses shared flags and passes them down
4. Clean separation between command routing, flag parsing, and execution

## Reference
- gh CLI help structure (see bead notes for full examples)
- Current implementation: lib/sift/cli/queue.rb

## Planning
Use this bead to design the new architecture before implementing.
close_reason: Refactored CLI to subcommand-per-class architecture: Base class with OptionParser flag chaining, QueueCommand root with 5 extracted subcommands (Add, Edit, List, Show, Rm), shared Formatters module, HelpRenderer for gh-style help, and per-command test files.

title: Add session continuity for revise
description: When user types revision feedback, call claude --resume <session_id> with the feedback. Update stored session_id. Show new response. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics'
close_reason: Already implemented in ReviewLoop#revise_analysis: resumes session with --resume flag, updates stored session_id, shows revised analysis

title: sq: Fix persistence/durability issues
description:
Persistence problems in the queue:

1. No atomic writes — write_all opens with 'w' which truncates before writing. A crash mid-write loses everything. Should write to a temp file then rename (atomic on POSIX).
2. No backup or journaling mechanism.
3. Blank lines are silently skipped in all(), but a partial JSON line from an interrupted append raises JSON::ParserError and makes the entire queue unreadable. Should handle gracefully (skip corrupt trailing line, log warning).

title: Research Sift as Roast wrapper gem
description: Explore pattern where Sift orchestrates Roast workflows externally. Sift manages queue/UI, calls roast execute programmatically, captures output, handles human review loop. May not need custom cog at all.
close_reason: Wrapper approach: Sift calls Roast::Workflow.from_file externally, queues results for review, runs separate workflows for revisions. Simpler than custom cog, loses session continuity. Recommend starting here per Sam's advice.

title: Enhance transcript source renderer
description: Enhance transcript source renderer for Claude conversations. Current Source::Transcript does basic speaker detection (H/A/System prefix) with ANSI colors and indentation. Missing: (1) markdown rendering in assistant responses - Claude outputs markdown with code blocks, lists, bold, etc. that currently show as raw text, (2) tool use blocks - Claude transcripts often include tool_use/tool_result that should be rendered distinctly, (3) word wrapping that respects the width parameter, (4) timestamp display if available, (5) message count/summary header. The speaker grouping logic is solid but the content rendering needs work for real Claude conversations.

title: Add CLI review command
description: Add a main CLI entry point that launches the queue-driven review loop. Currently the CLI module only has QueueCommand (add/edit/list/show/rm) but no command to actually start reviewing. Need a 'sift review' or 'sift start' command that: (1) loads the queue from the configured path (.sift/queue.jsonl), (2) launches the refactored ReviewLoop with queue integration, (3) shows a summary of pending items before starting, (4) handles the empty queue case (show waiting message or exit). This depends on the ReviewLoop queue integration (2026-02-03-roast-loop-b9z).

title: CLI queue management
description:
Full-featured CLI for queue operations:

## Commands
- `sift queue add` - Add item with sources
- `sift queue edit <id>` - Edit item fields via flags  
- `sift queue list` - List items (filterable by status)
- `sift queue show <id>` - Display item details
- `sift queue rm <id>` - Remove item

## Add command flags
```
--diff <path>        Add diff source (can repeat)
--file <path>        Add file source (can repeat)
--text <string>      Add text source (can repeat)
--transcript <path>  Add transcript source
--stdin <type>       Read source from stdin (diff|file|text)
--metadata <json>    Attach metadata
```

## Edit command flags
```
--add-diff <path>    Add diff source
--add-file <path>    Add file source
--rm-source <index>  Remove source by index
--set-status <s>     Change status
--set-metadata <json>
```

## Examples
```bash
# Add diff from stdin
git diff src/foo.rb | sift queue add --stdin diff

# Add item with multiple sources
sift queue add --diff src/foo.rb --file src/foo_test.rb --text 'Review this refactor'

# Edit existing item
sift queue edit abc123 --add-file README.md --set-metadata '{"priority": 1}'
```

Depends on: n89 (queue foundation)
close_reason: CLI queue management: add/edit/list/show/rm subcommands, stdin support, JSON output, repeatable source flags. Refactored exe/sift for subcommands.
